title: International GLOBOCAN/CI5 comparison (WHO World Standard)
context: BACKLOG: An international comparison using IARC GLOBOCAN and/or IARC CI5 data adds context to the US disparity analysis by situating domestic rates in a global frame. However, IARC/GLOBOCAN license terms may conflict with CC-BY-4.0 (IARC terms may restrict commercial redistribution of derived data); the per-artifact resolution documented in disparities-analysis-licensing-003 must be honored. Age-standardization uses the WHO World Standard Population. IARC reuse terms must be verified and the CC-BY conflict resolved per-artifact before any GLOBOCAN-derived data is published.
objective: Produce an international comparison dataset and summary using IARC GLOBOCAN and/or IARC Cancer Incidence in Five Continents (CI5) data for the exemplar cancer site, age-standardized to the WHO World Standard Population. Honor IARC reuse terms: for any source whose terms restrict redistribution of derived data, publish the analysis method and a pointer to the source rather than the restricted derived data, or apply the source-mandated terms to that specific artifact. Record the per-artifact license resolution. Apply the same suppression and provenance standards as all other project outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses IARC GLOBOCAN and/or CI5 data for the exemplar cancer site with age-standardization to the WHO World Standard Population

IARC reuse terms are verified before any data is used; the per-artifact license resolution is recorded (CC-BY-4.0 where permitted; for restricted artifacts: publish method + pointer or apply source-mandated NC/attribution terms)
No GLOBOCAN/CI5-derived data is published under CC-BY-4.0 if IARC terms prohibit it; in that case the published artifact is the method and source pointer, not the restricted data
International comparison is age-standardized to the WHO World Standard Population; standard population is recorded in all Source records
Suppression rules are applied (cross-check with source's own suppression policy); provenance records are complete
Results contextualize the US disparity findings within an international frame using evidence-tiered framing; no causal claims from ecological international comparisons
output: An international comparison dataset/summary for the exemplar site using GLOBOCAN/CI5 (WHO-standardized), with IARC license resolution documented per-artifact (CC-BY-4.0 where permitted; method + pointer for restricted artifacts).
outputLicense: CC-BY-4.0 where source license permits; IARC/GLOBOCAN artifacts: per-source terms apply
